title: How to Calculate Your FIRE Number
subtitle: A practical walkthrough of the 4% rule and personalization
description: Learn how to use the 4% rule to determine precisely how much you need to retire. Complete with examples of target portfolios and Lean FIRE annual expenses.

faqs.question: What is a FIRE number?
faqs.answer: A FIRE number is the amount of invested money you need to cover annual expenses without relying on a traditional paycheck.

faqs.question: How do you calculate your FIRE number?
faqs.answer: The simplest FIRE number formula is annual expenses multiplied by 25, which is based on a 4% withdrawal rate.

faqs.question: Should early retirees use a lower withdrawal rate?
faqs.answer: Early retirees often use a lower withdrawal rate, such as 3.5%, because their portfolios may need to last longer than a traditional 30-year retirement.
